frameworks.name: predictive feedback / hierarchical visual processing
frameworks.one_line: Higher visual areas send object-specific feedback to early visual cortex during integration.

frameworks.name: dynamic causal modeling
frameworks.one_line: Effective connectivity framework used to infer directed inter-areal modulations from fMRI.

frameworks.name: perceptual grouping / illusory figure completion
frameworks.one_line: Phenomenon framing: parts integrated into a coherent whole object.

core_question: Do feedback connections from lateral occipital cortex to V1/V2 underlie illusory figure completion?
key_assumptions: Illusory figures probe object integration mechanisms
DCM on fMRI can reveal directed inter-areal causal influence
LOC indexes mid-level object representation; V1/V2 indexes early visual processing
manipulations: Task: target-dot localization vs luminance discrimination
Task relevance of the configuration (relevant vs not)
key_findings: Behavioural benefit from integrated object only when configuration was task-relevant (localization)
Object-specific feedback connections from LOC to V1/V2 identified via DCM
Modulatory feedback persisted across both task demands and hemispheres
interpretations: Mid-level (LOC) to early-visual (V1/V2) interactions drive illusory figure perception
Object completion involves causal top-down modulation independent of immediate task set
